prompt: I want to make a SaaS product landing page. Show me 3 style directions to compare.
expected: Triggers clarifying questions about design context/brand; produces 3 variations via design-canvas.jsx; no purple gradients/emoji/Inter-as-display slop; specific rationale for each variation's differentiating dimension

prompt: Help me make a 10-page product pitch deck for an AI tool startup.
expected: Uses deck-stage.js; vocalizes design system (colors/fonts/layout rhythm) and waits for confirmation; alternates section divider/content/data/quote layouts; font size >= 24px; 1-indexed slide labels

prompt: Create a 30-second HTML animation explaining how neural networks work.
expected: Uses animations.jsx Stage+Sprite; writes timeline before components; entrance easeOut / exit easeIn; phases tell a story rather than stacking animations; text stays on screen >= 3 seconds

prompt: Design a Habit Tracker App prototype.
expected: Asks user for overview layout vs flow demo (defaults to overview); uses ios-frame.jsx — never hand-writes Dynamic Island; tracker is a high-density type, each screen has >= 3 information density elements (completion rate, streak count, trend chart, achievement badges — not decorative); at least 5-7 screens side-by-side (Home/New Habit/Detail/Statistics/Settings)

prompt: Design a Reading Notes App prototype.
expected: Overview layout by default; uses ios-frame.jsx; reading notes is content-display focused, information density is less extreme than a tracker but note list screen still needs >= 3 information layers (book, quotes, tags, progress); at least 4-6 screens (Bookshelf/Note Detail/Highlight Annotation/Search/Notebook Management); typography prioritizes serif display font

prompt: Design a Running Tracker App prototype.
expected: Overview layout; uses ios-frame.jsx; running app is high-density type (map, pace curve, heart rate zones, per-km split data), each screen has >= 3 product-differentiating data points; at least 5 screens (Today Overview/Live Run Data/Route Map/History/Monthly Stats); avoids AI slop (no purple gradients, no decorative icons, but data visualization icons are acceptable)

prompt: Make me a nice website for my coffee shop.
expected: Triggers design-direction-advisor (vague brief); asks for brand assets (logo, photos); generates 3 directions from different design schools; waits for user to choose before proceeding; uses core-asset-protocol if brand name given

prompt: Create a design for Stripe's new pricing page.
expected: Triggers core-asset-protocol (specific brand mentioned); searches for Stripe logo/brand assets; extracts colors from Stripe.com; writes brand-spec.md before starting design; uses real Stripe visual identity, not generic fintech aesthetic

prompt: Export my design as a video with background music.
expected: Uses ExportVideo tool; calls render-video.js with --json flag; if BGM requested, chains add-music.sh; returns structured JSON with output_path

prompt: Review and score this design mockup.
expected: Uses critique-guide 5-dimension model; scores each dimension 0-10 with rationale; provides 3 specific fixes per dimension; runs anti-slop check; assigns letter grade from total /50
